1381.name: Second Floor Landing
1381.description: You find yourself at the end of a long hall, covered with mouldering, dusty carpet and lined with glowing rods of diamond. At your feet are several boards covering a staircase leading to the first floor. The staircase continues winding up around the northeast tower.

1385.name: Second Floor Landing
1385.description: The stairs curve down in a graceful arc, wide and shallow. About halfway down, the goblins have erected a barricade of broken furniture, rubble, and rough-hewn wooden planks. The balustrade leads north and south. Diamond rods line the upper walls.

1387.name: Sitting Room
1387.description: An ancient, cracked coffee table sits before a set of matching chairs. The chairs face a window on the eastern wall, and you can see the trail leading up to the fortress. A stairway around the edge of the room climbs up to the southwest tower and is blocked in the downward direction by a trap door. A doorway leads north.

1382.name: Cold Hallway
1382.description: Sometimes you can catch a whiff of the chaos and stench of the rooms below, but for the most part all remains silent and well-ordered. You are surprised to see no rats or roaches here...or skeletons of the same. Rods light the way east and west, and doorways go north and south.

1383.name: Cold Hallway
1383.description: A long, cold hallway constructed of iron grey stone extends to the east and ends in a balustrade to the west. The stone is exposed except for a threadbare carpet housing centuries of dust. Diamond rods along the walls illuminate the way with a cold light. The library lies to the north.

1384.name: Balustrade
1384.description: A marble balustrade runs the length of the balcony overlooking the foyer. Constructed from the same grey stone as the rest of the fortress, it seems to blend in with its surroundings. A finger run along the top comes away black with dust. The balustrade runs north and south, and diamond rods line the walls.

1386.name: Cold Hallway
1386.description: A carved balustrade overlooks the foyer. Diamond rods illuminate the grey stone below in a singularly bleak light. Piles of garbage send up a pungent odor, and you can hear the buzzing of flies. To the south is a sitting room, to the east a hallway, and to the north, a stairway leading down.

1664.name: Side Tunnel
1664.description: A tunnel leads away from the mine shaft, heading west and down. The floor is littered with small rocks and the occasional bit of ore. You hear tapping coming from the west.

1665.name: Guard Room
1665.description: Most of the southern wall has been blasted away by some explosion. Chunks of masonry litter the marble floor, converted now to makeshift tables. Clubs and cudgels lie piled in a corner, along with leather jerkins that look like they could move on their own. A staircase winds up to the fortress tower, and archways lead to the east and south. The twisted remains of a chandelier hang from the ceiling.

1666.name: On the Staircase
1666.description: The goblins have boarded over this staircase as it passes through the floor of the second level.

1657.name: Tunnel
1657.description: The tunnel lets off abruptly about fifteen feet away from the mine shaft. The rock is loose and porous here, and rubble to the west indicates a collapse. You see a small fissure to the south, and the tunnel leads back east.

1661.name: Tunnel
1661.description: Supported by wooden beams harvested from the surrounding forests, this tunnel is strewn with recently mined debris. From the west you can hear the careful tapping of goblin miners. An opening leads south and the mine shaft lies to the east. The southern tunnel has been covered with boulders.

1637.name: Spiral Staircase
1637.description: The air feels decidedly damp and chilly. An occasional globe lights the gloom, but you cannot see more than a few feet in front of you. The stairway spirals around the tower interior, wrapping around manacled skeletons dangling in the center.

1638.name: Spiral Staircase
1638.description: The smell from an enclosed, underground area filled with goblins wafts up in nearly palpable waves. The stairway continues its descent around the tower walls.

1470.name: The Throne of Alestar
1470.description: You behold an immense throne carved from a single block of obsidian. Skulls dipped in copper dangle from the corners and a few filthy pillows cover the seat. The throne appears solidly, sternly immovable, despite its showy trappings.

npcs.name: Hox
npcs.description: shifts his weight on the pillows.

1388.name: Spiral Staircase
1388.description: The staircase winds up and around the southwest tower. Occasional windows let you catch a glimpse of the mountainous terrain below. A doorway stands at the top of the stairs. Diamond rods illuminate the walls.

1647.name: Treasure Vault
1647.description: Recently mined chunks of copper ore and garnets of all sizes litter the floor of this room. Though not a great treasure, the contents of this room ensure the prosperity of the Dagaar tribe for months to come.

1640.name: Damp Corridor
1640.description: Smoky torches gutter down this passageway filling it with a murky haze. Lined with cell doors, it leads east to the guard room and west to more cells.

1641.name: Damp Passageway
1641.description: It looks and smells like your typical dungeon. That's not saying much, either. Cells sit to either side.

1644.name: Cell
1644.description: The southern wall of this cell has been chipped away, forming a much larger chamber. A mine shaft descends down into darkness, and you can hear faint tapping far below. Pulleys supporting large chains allow the goblins to extract large amounts of earth.

1642.name: Damp Passageway
1642.description: The passageway branches here, probably leading to more cells. The goblins, however, are in the process of filling these with debris. A bright room lies to the east, and the passage continues west. Kick is no longer on cooldown!

1639.name: Guard Room
1639.description: The odors of stale beer, rotting food and poor sanitation mix with the more subtle scents of fear and blood. Various cudgels and clubs lie here, as well as pick axes, lanterns and chisels. A door leads east to the dungeons, and a stairway leads up. A heavy door bars the stairway as it continues down.

1643.name: Lower Laboratory
1643.description: A large table with rusted manacles sits in the center of this whitewashed room. Glowing globes suffuse the area with a hard, brilliant light. Various pieces of rusted machinery line the walls, their purpose forgotten. The goblins have since molded this room to their own purposes, turning it into an interrogation chamber.

1645.name: Mine Shaft
1645.description: A rough, wooden ladder leads down the shaft, which is wide enough to allow a system of pulleys to bring up rubble and debris. A small tunnel branches off and goes east. A narrow seam of copper glints from the rock face.

1648.name: Mine Shaft
1648.description: The mine shaft leads down into the heart of the mountain. Sometimes, the occasional small rock tumbles down from above and seconds pass until you hear it strike bottom below. You hear the tapping of hammers and the chink of shattering rock.

1649.name: Mine Shaft
1649.description: A large tunnel has been carved out at this level, running east and west. The mine shaft continues to descend.

1646.name: Side Tunnel
1646.description: This tunnel leads from the main shaft to a small room to the north. Torches upon the walls gutter and flare, casting shifting, horrific shadows upon the rock.

1650.name: Side Tunnel
1650.description: A tunnel cuts across the downward angle of the mine shaft, heading east through the mountain rock. On the way, you see discarded lanterns, a broken pick or two, and a bit of wooden beam.

1653.name: Fissure Bottom
1653.description: The fissure isn't very wide, and at some points, you would be wedged in between the walls. You could probably make your way south, however, in the direction where a skull has been mounted on a stick driven into the ground. A pale metallic shimmer catches the light.

1654.name: By an Underground Pool
1654.description: Inky waters form a flat, placid surface across the rough stone. A stone cast into the center disappears with a deep *ploop*, but other than that, you get no indication of its depth.

1655.name: Mine Shaft
1655.description: You feel the top and sides of the mountain pressing against you. Is it your imagination, or is the mine shaft less wide than you had first thought? The ladder leads upward and down to the mine bottom.

1656.name: Mine Bottom
1656.description: Gravel covers the bottom of the mine shaft, remains of the debris the goblins have removed. A small tunnel leads west. Above the faraway din of mining goblins you can hear your own breath in your ears. Dull gray tin flecks are scattered through the stone.

1651.name: Side Tunnel
1651.description: In the damp, chilly darkness, you hear the tapping of miners from all around. Sometimes you catch an odd phrase as one goblin speaks to another, but the sounds are disjointed and faraway. The tunnel leads west and north.

1658.name: Natural Tunnel
1658.description: The tunnel lets off abruptly about fifteen feet away from the mine shaft. The rock is loose and porous here, and rubble to the west indicates a collapse. You see a small fissure to the south, and the tunnel leads back east.

1659.name: Natural Tunnel
1659.description: Granite and quartz form ripples along the sides and roof of the tunnel, which twists and winds through the mountain. At one point you are forced to crawl through a particularly low overhang. The tunnel leads east and up.

1660.name: Large Cavern
1660.description: The internal moisture of the mountain has condensed upon the ceiling here, dripping down to form stalactites and stalagmites gradually through the ages. Goblin bones litter the area, and your nostrils catch a whiff of danger. A tunnel blocked by boulders leads north and a hole leads down. Dull gray tin flecks are scattered through the stone.

1652.name: Side Tunnel
1652.description: The tunnel meets an internal fissure here, running to the edge and then moving away to the north. Peering down, you cannot see the bottom, but a stone thrown over the edge assures you that it's not far.

1662.name: Tunnel
1662.description: Following a vein of coppery ore, the goblins have snaked their way through the mountain. The tunnel ends here, where it seems work is still in progress. A cluster of mushrooms glows with an eerie red light, pulsing faintly. A narrow seam of copper glints from the rock face.

1663.name: Slope
1663.description: Following a vein of coppery ore, the goblins have snaked their way through the mountain. The tunnel ends here, where it seems work is still in progress.

1380.name: Private Dining Room
1380.description: A long trestle table sits in the middle of this room, laden with the remains of a large meal. Tarnished and mismatched silverware lies scattered over the surface, and a plate of gnawed bones still serves as the centerpiece. A blocked off stairway leads up to the second floor, and a doorway leads east to the great hall.

1464.name: Corridor
1464.description: A corridor runs from a large room to the west, branching north or heading east. The stench of refuse, urine and sweat roils from the western direction, apparently a common living area for the goblins.

1465.name: Corridor
1465.description: Running along the southern wall of Alestar's fortress, this passageway reeks of goblins. A cold draft blows from west to east, and the walls are bathed in perpetual shadow.

1468.name: Great Hall
1468.description: Stately pillars rise up to support a ceiling fallen away to reveal the sky above. Rubble and debris cover the tiled marble floor, and enormous flying buttresses reach across the open sky like the ribs of a decayed corpse. The goblins have draped the walls and rest of the ceiling with soiled silks of red and orange and gold.

1469.name: Before the Throne
1469.description: A massive throne sits upon an ebony dias, covered with garish red pillows and backed by a copper panel carved to resemble flames. Behold the hall of ruined splendor! Even tawdry and gutted, its interior gilt and its ribs exposed to the elements, you feel a sense of weight pressing downward, of power vast as the universe, as elemental as the earth itself. A large blackened area lies about 20 feet from the throne and a little to its left. An eagle-emblazoned totem pulses with a serene, mystical energy.

1467.name: Small Room
1467.description: Looks like the goblins use this room as a supply dump. You see several casks of foul beer, a few jugs of wine, mealy flour, and dried beef. Roaches scurry over the ground at your feet, and you can hear the occasional rat squeaking. Doorways lead north and south. You aren't very hungry.

1365.name: Rocky Trail
1365.description: A thin, rocky trail winds its way up through the rocks on the mountain. It switches back on itself with sharp, sudden turns, sometimes sloping as much as sixty degrees. The bones crunch brittly beneath your feet, like dry sticks. Below, a thick, unnatural fog covers the trail.

1366.name: Rocky Trail
1366.description: Miserable shrubs cling precariously to the mountainous terrain. The trail leads steeply back and forth, and far far above you can see a grim fortress squatting on the summit like a hideous bird of prey.

1367.name: Rocky Trail
1367.description: A level area, perhaps large enough for ten men to stand comfortably, marks the end of the upward trail. An immense chasm yawns to the immediate east, spanned by a bridge of single but massive stones. A monument sits by the causeway hung with various skulls.

1368.name: Causeway
1368.description: Wind shrieks ominously around the causeway as currents from the chasm and mountaintop clash. The fortress of Torm Sarvek looms to the east, its sharp angles cutting a fearsomely majestic silhouette against the sky. Looking down, you can see no bottom.

e.to_room_name: At the Gates of Torm Sarvek

1369.name: At the Gates of Torm Sarvek
1369.description: You are struck by the massive size of this place, its austere lines and its stark facades. The gates have long been shattered by some great force, but the archway rises high above you, large enough to allow giants easy passage. You can detect a strong odor coming from within.

1370.name: Courtyard
1370.description: Piles of garbage lay against the outer walls, rotting and stinking with an odor almost visible in their repugnance. Flies swarm everywhere, so numerous their buzzing creates a roar. The shattered gate lies to the west and a broken fountain to the north. The towers of Torm Sarvek rear up against the eastern sky.

w.to_room_name: At the Gates of Torm Sarvek

1371.name: Courtyard
1371.description: Centuries ago, a great battle raged here as the forces of Ilarien cut a swath of destruction through Alestar's armies of undead. It is said that while the armies clashed, Ilarien went on alone through the inner gate and challenged the then Emperor of the World to a final duel. You would not think that such grand events occurred here amidst this fly-infested slumhole.

1372.name: Foyer
1372.description: Built by the mad mage Alestar, Torm Sarvek now houses the tribe of Dagaar. Large archways lead north, south and east. The entrance lies to the west, and a barricade of rubble and wood blocks a stairway to the second floor. Even after all these years, the fortress still wears a sense of immensity and grandeur-- despite the garbage littering the floor and the squalid odor permeating the halls.

1373.name: Long Corridor
1373.description: Supported by smooth, round pillars, many now cracked, this corridor runs east and west from the foyer to an intersection. Scarred and knicked bas reliefs cover the walls, and globes affixed to the walls with iron claws glow with a nacreous light. This passage must have been an imposing sight indeed to an approaching prisoner. Now it is filled with trash.

1374.name: Intersection
1374.description: Four archways lead to the north, south, east and west. The rest of the walls are bare and grim, lit sporadically by cracked globes. Pieces of food litter the floor, slowly putrifying with age.

1376.name: Corridor
1376.description: A corridor of iron grey stone stretches out to the north and south. Covered now with dust and strewn with garbage, you can almost imagine how it would have looked long ago, in the height of Alestar's power, how the very walls seemed to rise up and belittle you with their geometry.

1377.name: Corridor
1377.description: A corridor runs across the northern part of the fortress, running east to west and branching south. A door creaking back and forth on rusted hinges leads out to an overgrown garden. The dim illumination produced from cracked globes on the walls does little to chase away the shadows.

1378.name: Corridor
1378.description: Rats squeak and scurry into the shadows. You see rat bones and half eaten carcasses against the wall, left to rot where they lie. The corridor runs from the east to west.

1379.name: Corridor
1379.description: Built on a grand scale, the sheer immensity of this place manifests itself in even the corridors, which are broad enough for five men and higher than two. Brackets in the form of claws house crystal globes, some glowing and some defunct. The way lies to the east and west.

1635.name: Guard Room
1635.description: Probably once a sitting room, it now serves as a guard station. A mouldering carpet breathes clouds of dust, and a vaulted ceiling supports two chandeliers now blackened with grime and age. Skulls of various foes hang from them like mobiles, swaying softly with the air currents. Archways lead north and east, and a staircase climbs up and down.

1636.name: Living Area
1636.description: A great painting covers the southern wall, filmed with soot and cracked with age. Huddled forms of goblins sprawl upon the ground where once the mighty Alestar ruled. A fetid odor wafts through the room, its source everywhere about you. Archways lead to the north and east.
